user_task: Nemo Retriever library setup on local GPU machine: GPU detected (automatic detection) — Extraction, Embedding, Reranking models load directly on a local GPU.
source: JTBD & user tasks tab, SETUP bucket, row 2; seed queries from 'NeMo Retriever — JTBD seed queries' tab, SETUP → 'Setup on local GPU machine — models loaded directly on local GPU'.

success_criteria.quality: operational pass — (1) clean library install with models loaded directly on local GPU, (2) ingest a PDF with models downloaded/loaded locally, (3) first successful retrieval served by GPU-resident models, all binary pass/fail
success_criteria.time: end-to-end (clean state → first successful retrieval) <= 30 minutes
success_criteria.trigger_rate: >= 95% — an on-device setup-and-load prompt must fire the skill
success_criteria.subcommand_accuracy: >= 90% — must run `retriever ingest` then `retriever query` with LOCAL-GPU flags (local backends, no --embed-invoke-url / --api-key / hosted endpoints)

environment.install: pip/uv install '.[local]' on a CUDA-13 host (GPU torch wheels from the cu130 index); see skills/nemo-retriever/references/install.md
environment.no_hosted: No NVIDIA_API_KEY / hosted invoke-urls needed for the smoke loop. Embedding + reranking run on the bundled GPU models; text extraction is local pdfium. The smoke run must make ZERO outbound calls to ai.api.nvidia.com / integrate.api.nvidia.com / build.nvidia.com.
environment.backend_note: Query uses --query-embed-backend hf + --reranker-backend hf (HuggingFace, ~20-30s cold start, GPU-resident) for one-off CLI queries; ingest uses the default vLLM backend for batch throughput. Both load the model on the local GPU.
environment.extraction_caveat: Text-PDF extraction (pdfium) is fully local on CPU — no NIM call, so a text-only smoke is 100% on-device. Running page-elements / OCR / table-structure on the LOCAL GPU (not the hosted ai.api.nvidia.com default) requires either local NIM endpoints (--page-elements-invoke-url http://localhost:... etc.) or batch run-mode with GPU actors (--run-mode batch --page-elements-gpus-per-actor / --ocr-gpus-per-actor / --table-structure-gpus-per-actor). This is the genuinely harder part of the row and is what case 004 targets.

cases.title: On-device smoke loop: [local] install + GPU detect + single text-PDF ingest → query
cases.complexity_axis: Baseline happy path, GPU flavor: install with [local], confirm a GPU is visible, then close the ingest→query loop on one trivial text PDF using local backends. Text extraction is local pdfium, so this run touches no network at all.
cases.prompt: I have a CUDA-capable GPU. Install with local GPU support, confirm the models loaded onto the card, then load data/test.pdf and tell me what the document says — keep everything on-device, no hosted calls.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "What does the document say?" --top-k 5 --embed-model-name nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2 --query-embed-backend hf --reranker-backend hf --rerank

expected_output.install: uv pip install '.[local]' (cu130 torch) completes (<= 20 min)
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) → N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of hits; each {source, page_number, text}; >= 1 hit citing test.pdf p1
expected_output.answer: placeholder text + an image reference; cite test.pdf p1

pass_when: [local] install succeeds and a GPU is detected
ingest + query round-trip with LOCAL backends (no --embed-invoke-url / --api-key)
no outbound build.nvidia.com / ai.api.nvidia.com / integrate.api.nvidia.com call in the trace

cases.title: Confirm models resident in VRAM + zero hosted egress
cases.complexity_axis: Adds the two explicit GPU-setup assertions the row calls for: after load, the model(s) actually occupy VRAM (nvidia-smi memory), and the ingest+query made NO outbound call to a hosted NVIDIA endpoint. Exercises the 'confirm the models loaded onto the card' seed query.
cases.prompt: Set up on my GPU box and confirm the embedding/reranker models are actually resident on the card (not calling the cloud). Then load data/woods_frost.pdf and tell me who owns the woods.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "Who owns the woods?" --top-k 5 --embed-model-name nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2 --query-embed-backend hf --reranker-backend hf --rerank
nvidia-smi  (VRAM occupied by the python/retriever process during/after the query)

expected_output.vram_assertion: nvidia-smi shows the retriever process holding GPU memory (embedder + reranker loaded)
expected_output.egress_assertion: no request to ai.api.nvidia.com / integrate.api.nvidia.com / build.nvidia.com in the trace
expected_output.answer: The woods' owner's 'house is in the village' — the owner lives in the village; cite woods_frost.pdf p1

pass_when: GPU memory is occupied by the local models (VRAM check passes)
zero hosted egress during ingest + query

cases.title: Local GPU reranking over a 3-PDF folder
cases.complexity_axis: Adds folder-level ingest and puts the spotlight on the local GPU RERANKER (the third model class the row requires on-device). The reranked order must differ from dense-only order, and the reranker must run via the local --reranker-backend hf, not a hosted endpoint.
cases.prompt: On my GPU machine, load the whole data/ folder and find — best match first, reranked on-device — which Robert Frost collection was published in 1923.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "Robert Frost collection published in 1923" --top-k 5 --embed-model-name nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2 --query-embed-backend hf --reranker-backend hf --rerank

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) → N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary pdf:3)
expected_output.answer: New Hampshire (1923); cite woods_frost.pdf p2 (collection table)
expected_output.rerank_note: --reranker-backend hf loads the local GPU reranker; reranked order != dense-only order; no hosted reranker call

pass_when: reranker runs locally (--reranker-backend hf) — no ai.api.nvidia.com reranking call
correct collection (New Hampshire) at/near top, citing woods_frost.pdf p2

cases.title: Local GPU visual extraction (page-elements / OCR / table on the card)
cases.complexity_axis: The genuinely hard clause: making EXTRACTION run on the local GPU instead of the hosted ai.api.nvidia.com default. A table PDF forces page-elements → OCR → table-structure. The agent must route these to local GPU (local NIM endpoints OR batch run-mode with --*-gpus-per-actor) so the no-hosted-egress invariant still holds while a table cell is recovered.
cases.prompt: Everything must stay on my GPU — no cloud calls, including the document parsing. Load data/table_test.pdf on-device and tell me what value James had in 2019.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER ingest data/table_test.pdf --run-mode batch --page-elements-gpus-per-actor 1 --ocr-gpus-per-actor 1 --table-structure-gpus-per-actor 1   # OR pin local NIM endpoints via --page-elements-invoke-url/--ocr-invoke-url/--table-structure-invoke-url http://localhost:...
$RETRIEVER query "James value in 2019" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table --embed-model-name nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2 --query-embed-backend hf --reranker-backend hf --rerank

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) → N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (table rows present)
expected_output.egress_assertion: no ai.api.nvidia.com extraction call — page-elements/OCR/table ran on the local GPU (or a localhost NIM)

pass_when: extraction (page-elements/OCR/table) runs on the local GPU or a localhost NIM — NOT the hosted default
table rows present; query returns 978 citing table_test.pdf p1
cases.known_caveat: A bare '.[local]' pip install gives GPU embedding + reranking out of the box; on-device VISUAL extraction additionally needs the local GPU extraction actors (batch run-mode) or local NIM endpoints. If neither is configured, default extraction silently falls back to the hosted ai.api.nvidia.com endpoints — which this test is designed to catch as a fail of the 'extraction on local GPU' clause.

cases.title: Full operational-pass acceptance: all 3 model classes on GPU, fresh named index, citation, no egress, <= 30 min
cases.complexity_axis: Composed acceptance gate for the row: a clean [local] install → ingest into a custom-named index (table name aligned across ingest AND query) → a cited answer, while asserting that embedding, reranking AND extraction all ran on the local GPU (VRAM), zero outbound build.nvidia.com calls occurred, and the whole thing finished in <= 30 min.
cases.prompt: Do a clean on-device setup on this GPU machine: install local GPU support, load data/ into a fresh index called 'gpu_smoke', then answer with a citation — in which poem does the speaker say they have 'miles to go before I sleep'? Confirm embedding, reranking, and extraction all ran on the GPU with no cloud calls, and that it finished under 30 minutes.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "miles to go before I sleep" --table-name gpu_smoke --top-k 5 --embed-model-name nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2 --query-embed-backend hf --reranker-backend hf --rerank

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) → N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/gpu_smoke.
expected_output.answer: 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening' by Robert Frost; cite woods_frost.pdf p1
assertions: query reads the SAME table (gpu_smoke) the ingest wrote — not the default nemo-retriever
every returned hit carries non-null source + page_number
embedding + reranking + extraction all occupied VRAM (nvidia-smi)
zero outbound calls to ai.api.nvidia.com / integrate.api.nvidia.com / build.nvidia.com
wall clock (clean state → query answer) <= 30 min

pass_when: ingest writes lancedb/gpu_smoke and query reads gpu_smoke (table names aligned)
all three model classes ran on the local GPU; no hosted egress; end-to-end <= 30 min
